response.justification: Bogdan is a highly accomplished Staff/Senior Software Developer with nearly 20 years of experience, which creates a severe seniority mismatch for a role explicitly described as entry-level. While his TypeScript, Node.js, and clean architecture credentials are strong, his primary database experience is MongoDB rather than PostgreSQL, NestJS is absent from his history, and his current trajectory (Staff Engineer, AI infrastructure, MCP frameworks) is far beyond the scope of this position. Retaining him at this level would be implausible, and his salary expectations would almost certainly exceed the budget.
strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js expertise across high-scale production systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby, millions of mail sends at Optilyz)
Strong clean-architecture and engineering discipline: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pairing — directly matching the role's 'interest in clean software architecture' requirement
Several nice-to-have qualifications covered: LLM/agentic workflows (re:cinq), Kubernetes (GISCollective), and broad AWS-adjacent cloud infrastructure experience
concerns: Severe overqualification — Staff/Senior developer with ~20 years of experience applying for an entry-level individual contributor role; retention risk is near-certain
Relational database experience is thin — MongoDB dominates his history; PostgreSQL is not mentioned, and SQL/MySQL appear only as peripheral skill-list entries
NestJS is absent from all roles; React appears in one position (Optilyz) but is not a prominent thread in his career, which is weighted toward backend and systems work
